title: The grandmother who lived above us in Lisbon
summary: A short portrait of an elderly neighbour upstairs who became part of daily life.
topic: The grandmother who lived above us in Lisbon
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about an older neighbour who lived near you. Describe what she did, what you talked about, and how she affected your life. Use past simple and 'used to'.
hintStarters: When I rented a small flat in…, the woman above me was…
She used to knock three times on the floor whenever…
Although we did not share a language well, we…
sampleText: When I rented a small flat in Lisbon for a year, the woman who lived above me was a widow called Dona Conceição. She was almost eighty and walked slowly because of a weak ankle, but she still climbed the four flights of stairs every day. We met in the entrance hall on my second morning, when she was carrying a heavy bag of oranges. I helped her up to her door, and from then on she treated me like a member of her family. She used to knock three times on the floor whenever her soup was ready, and I would go upstairs to taste it. Her kitchen smelled of garlic and coriander, and the blue tiles around her sink were older than my country. She did not speak much English, and my Portuguese was poor, but we were both patient. She showed me photographs of her husband, her three sons, and a small village in the north where she was born. Sometimes she sat on her balcony and watched the river for a long time without saying anything. I think she was a little lonely, but she never said so. The year I lived under her kitchen was one of the warmest of my life.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the woman who lived above me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: người phụ nữ sống ở tầng trên tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: climbed the four flights of stairs
treated me like a member of her family
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đối xử với tôi như một thành viên trong gia đình

sampleAnnotations.phrase: used to knock three times on the floor
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thường gõ ba lần lên sàn nhà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Her kitchen smelled of garlic and coriander
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Căn bếp của bà có mùi tỏi và ngò
cũ hơn cả đất nước của tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: watched the river for a long time
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngắm dòng sông một thời gian dài

sampleAnnotations.phrase: one of the warmest of my life
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một trong những năm ấm áp nhất đời tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Used to' for past habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Used to + verb' diễn tả một thói quen trong quá khứ mà bây giờ không còn nữa: 'She used to knock three times on the floor.' Đừng nhầm với 'be used to + V-ing' (đã quen với).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Used to + verb' describes a past habit that no longer happens: 'She used to knock three times on the floor.' Do not confuse it with 'be used to + V-ing' (be accustomed to).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Comparative: older than
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dạng so sánh hơn của tính từ ngắn dùng đuôi -er + than: 'older than my country', 'warmer than last year'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Short adjectives form comparatives with -er + than: 'older than my country', 'warmer than last year'.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi thuê một căn hộ nhỏ ở Lisbon trong một năm, người phụ nữ sống ở tầng trên tôi là một góa phụ tên là Dona Conceição. Bà gần tám mươi và đi lại chậm vì một mắt cá chân yếu, nhưng bà vẫn leo bốn tầng cầu thang mỗi ngày. Chúng tôi gặp nhau ở sảnh vào buổi sáng thứ hai của tôi, khi bà đang xách một túi cam nặng. Tôi giúp bà lên tận cửa, và từ đó bà đối xử với tôi như một thành viên trong gia đình. Bà thường gõ ba lần lên sàn nhà mỗi khi súp đã nấu xong, và tôi sẽ lên nếm thử. Căn bếp của bà có mùi tỏi và ngò, và những viên gạch men xanh quanh bồn rửa còn cũ hơn cả đất nước của tôi. Bà không nói nhiều tiếng Anh, và tiếng Bồ Đào Nha của tôi cũng kém, nhưng chúng tôi đều kiên nhẫn. Bà cho tôi xem ảnh chồng bà, ba người con trai, và một ngôi làng nhỏ ở phía bắc nơi bà sinh ra. Đôi khi bà ngồi trên ban công và ngắm dòng sông một thời gian dài mà không nói gì. Tôi nghĩ bà hơi cô đơn một chút, nhưng bà không bao giờ nói ra. Năm tôi sống dưới căn bếp của bà là một trong những năm ấm áp nhất đời tôi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past habit form.
options: She use to knock three times on the floor.
She used to knocked three times on the floor.
She used to knock three times on the floor.
She was used to knock three times on the floor.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to' + base verb is the standard past-habit form.

options: a woman whose husband has died
a woman who has never married
a woman who lives alone by choice
a woman who works in a kitchen
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'widow' is a woman whose husband has died.

options: The tiles were more old than my country.
The tiles were older than my country.
The tiles were older as my country.
The tiles were the oldest than my country.
She walked slowly because of a weak ankle.
She walked slowly because of she had a weak ankle.
She walked slowly because a weak ankle.
She walked slowly because of her ankle was weak.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because of' is followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause.

options: someone in a hospital
able to wait calmly without getting angry
very fast and skilled
rich and generous
mcQuestions.explanation: As an adjective, 'patient' means calm and tolerant.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence using 'flight(s) of stairs'.
options: She climbed the four floor of stair every day.
She climbed the four flights of stairs every day.
She climbed the four flight stairs every day.
She climbed the four staircase every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Flight of stairs' = a set of steps between floors; plural 'flights of stairs'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best meaning of 'a little lonely'.
options: completely alone with no one
slightly lonely, not very strongly
happy to be alone
afraid of other people

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows good past-simple use for a single event?
options: I helped her up to her door.
I was helping her up to her door.
I have helped her up to her door.
I am helping her up to her door.
mcQuestions.explanation: Single completed past action → past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple for the moment of helping.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'treated me like a member of her family' mean?
options: she behaved toward me as if I were family
she paid me money like family does
she avoided me to keep distance
she invited me to a doctor
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Treat someone like X' = behave toward them as if they were X.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'photographs ___ her husband'.
options: of
from
about
with

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence ending.
options: I think she was a little lonely, but she never said so.
I think she was a little lonely, but she never says so.
I think she is a little lonely, but she never said so.
I think she was a little lonely, but she never told.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses are in past simple; 'said so' is a natural fixed pattern.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the past tense across both clauses.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think older neighbours sometimes connect more easily with young people from abroad than with neighbours their own age?
